sentence_reviews.text: Behavior is learned from demonstrations, or the reward structure explaining demonstrations is inferred.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning'; RL book discussion of imitation/apprenticeship and inverse reinforcement learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse RL from demonstrations is directly supported by AIMA's topic listing; imitation learning from demonstrations is supported by standard RL references but not directly visible in the inspected AIMA line.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning is to connect Reinforcement learning, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences to downstream capabilities including Robotics & embodied AI.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning', 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning', and robotics/control chapters; RL book reinforcement-learning setup and inverse/imitation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: RL and inverse RL are supported; the bridge to the broad supervision-signals label and robotics downstream capability is plausible but not directly established by the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, robotics/control chapters and Chapter 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Robotics and inverse RL are both present in AIMA, but the inspected evidence does not directly state imitation/inverse RL as a substrate for robotics.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' with 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning' under the same chapter.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse reinforcement learning is directly included under the reinforcement-learning chapter; imitation learning is not directly named in the inspected source.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 reinforcement learning/inverse RL and Chapter 27 AI limits/safety; RL book reinforcement-learning setup.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies on RL and demonstration/reward signals are plausible; the generic deployment mismatch limitation is not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning follows the learning setup expressed by Reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' and 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse RL is explicitly presented as an RL topic; imitation learning is related through learning from demonstrations.

sentence_reviews.text: Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning'; RL book reward/demonstration discussion in inverse/imitation contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Demonstrations and inferred rewards are supported, but labels, targets, and preferences broaden the dependency label beyond direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 17 sequential decision problems and Chapter 22 reinforcement learning; RL book trajectories/episodes in RL.
sentence_reviews.notes: Demonstrations in RL contexts are naturally sequential trajectories; the exact atlas label is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Inverse RL is explicitly an RL subtopic; trained_under/core is defensible for the combined node.
Demonstrations and rewards are central, but the broader labels/targets/rewards/preferences dependency label is only partially supported.
Demonstrations and RL experience are sequence/trajectory based; uses/common is defensible.
